sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Health System
special_mechanics.description: Player starts with 3 health indicators (small red triangles). Each hit/fall/trap removes one. Maximum health can be increased by collecting large red potions.

special_mechanics.name: Potion System
special_mechanics.description: Red potions restore 1 health. Blue potions deal 1 damage. Large green potions grant temporary hover ability.

special_mechanics.name: Combat
special_mechanics.description: Player can advance, back off, slash, parry, or parry-then-slash. Enemies have health indicators. Can defeat enemies by depleting health or pushing into traps.

special_mechanics.name: Magic Mirror Doppelgänger
special_mechanics.description: Encountered in level 4. Creates a doppelgänger that drains protagonist health to 1. Returns in later levels to steal potions and hinder progress.

special_mechanics.name: Shadow Doppelgänger (Level 12)
special_mechanics.description: Cannot be killed; shares health with protagonist. Must be merged with to access invisible bridge and proceed to final boss.

special_mechanics.name: Gate Activation
special_mechanics.description: Gates can be raised briefly by standing on activation triggers. Player must pass through before they close.

tier_notes: Tier 1 data from Wikipedia contains gameplay mechanics but lacks precise movement physics parameters. No ROM addresses, hex values, or quantified movement data documented. Game uses rotoscoped animation from live-action reference footage of acrobatic stunts, suggesting realistic physics modeling but specific numerical parameters are not publicly documented.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Prince of Persia (1989 video game)
